stages.title: Start with the work that must succeed.
stages.lede: The engine, cache, and router only matter if they complete the same accepted task.

stages.title: The user typed one sentence. The harness added the iceberg.
stages.lede: Instructions, tool schemas, repository context, history, and tool results all enter the request path.

stages.title: Change context lifetime before changing hardware.
stages.lede: Keep stable instructions stable, load procedures on demand, and project large tool output into compact evidence.

stages.title: The right engine changes when the workload changes.
stages.lede: One private user on Apple Silicon and one hundred concurrent users on NVIDIA do not need the same runtime.

stages.title: Each sponsor performs one visible operation.
stages.lede: Butterbase persists evidence, EverOS changes the next lesson, and Nebius runs the production inference branch.

stages.title: Follow the request from text to hot accelerator state.
stages.lede: Tokens enter prefill, prefill creates KV, decode repeatedly reads weights and KV, and HBM holds the hot working set.

stages.title: Retrieve the useful case instead of replaying the whole trajectory.
stages.lede: EverOS compresses verified agent work into Cases and repeated procedures into Skills. The next run retrieves only the relevant memory and must still pass the same acceptance test.

stages.title: Separate the working product from the missing live proof.
stages.lede: Local tests can pass while sponsor credentials, provider usage, and production economics remain unknown.

stages.title: The next assignment ships a real improvement.
stages.lede: Reproduce one router mechanism, then resolve one bounded issue with code, tests, and a reviewable receipt.

workload.task: Fix the retry-state corruption bug without changing the public API. Run the acceptance tests and return a reviewable patch.
workload.acceptance: Independent retry calls do not share attempt state; all supplied tests pass.

optimizations.title: Preserve the stable prefix
optimizations.description: Keep system rules and tool order deterministic; move volatile state later.

optimizations.title: Move procedures into an on-demand skill
optimizations.description: Keep only universal facts in AGENTS.md or CLAUDE.md.

optimizations.title: Project large tool output
optimizations.description: Keep failing tests and relevant frames; store the full log as an artifact.

optimizations.title: Reuse verified repository memory
optimizations.description: Retain architecture facts and test commands, not temporary hypotheses.

learner_memory.misconception: A production serving engine must always be faster than a local runtime.
learner_memory.default_instruction: Pick the engine with the highest feature score.
learner_memory.changed_instruction: Rank privacy, setup cost, hardware fit, concurrency, and acceptance quality before runtime features. For one private Apple Silicon user, compare MLX-LM and llama.cpp before vLLM.
learner_memory.measurement_method: fixture characters divided by four; model tokenizer and provider usage remain unmeasured
learner_memory.vendor_claim: EverMind reports 7–15x lower token use and up to 90% reduction for its proprietary retrieval path; Touchdown has not independently reproduced that claim.
